meaning: vi.(逐步或暗中)侵占,蚕食,超出通常(或正常)界线
examples: I did not mean to encroach upon your property.
I won't encroach on your time any longer.
He never allows work to encroach upon his family life.
It involves setting up electric fences and relocating villages which encroach on the animals' migratory corridors.
Working-class whites do not, however, warm to those who seem to encroach on their privileges.
